name: Inset-Fed Patch (microstrip)
description: A rectangular microstrip patch antenna fed by a microstrip line inserted into the patch at a specific distance from the edge to achieve impedance matching. The inset feed eliminates the need for external matching networks by positioning the feed point at the desired input impedance location along the patch.

patch_length.name: Patch Length
patch_length.description: Length of the rectangular patch along the resonant dimension

patch_width.name: Patch Width
patch_width.description: Width of the rectangular patch perpendicular to the resonant dimension

substrate_permittivity.name: Relative Permittivity
substrate_permittivity.description: Relative dielectric constant of the substrate material

inset_distance.name: Inset Distance
inset_distance.description: Distance from the patch edge to the feed point for impedance matching

feedline_width.name: Feedline Width
feedline_width.description: Width of the microstrip feedline for 50-ohm characteristic impedance

designMethodology.overview: Design process involves calculating patch dimensions for resonance, determining effective permittivity, computing inset distance for impedance matching, and optimizing feedline geometry.

steps.title: Calculate Patch Width
steps.description: Determine optimal patch width for efficient radiation

steps.title: Calculate Effective Permittivity
steps.description: Compute effective dielectric constant accounting for fringing fields

steps.title: Calculate Extension Length
steps.description: Determine fringing field extension due to open-end effects

steps.title: Calculate Patch Length
steps.description: Determine resonant patch length including fringing effects

steps.title: Calculate Edge Resistance
steps.description: Determine input resistance at the radiating edge

steps.title: Calculate Inset Distance
steps.description: Determine inset distance for 50-ohm input impedance

steps.title: Design Feedline
steps.description: Calculate microstrip feedline width for 50-ohm impedance
steps.formula: Wf = 8*h*exp(A)/(exp(2*A)-2) where A depends on Z0 and epsilon_r

steps.title: Optimize Performance
steps.description: Fine-tune dimensions using simulation for desired bandwidth and matching
steps.formula: Adjust L, W, y0 based on S11 < -10 dB requirement

equations.gain: G = 4*pi*A_eff/lambda_0^2 where A_eff ≈ 0.8*W*L for rectangular patch

references: Balanis C.A. 'Antenna Theory: Analysis and Design' 4th Edition Chapter 14
Stutzman W.L. & Thiele G.A. 'Antenna Theory and Design' 3rd Edition Chapter 10
Volakis J.L. 'Antenna Engineering Handbook' 4th Edition Chapter 11
